scenario_name: Scenario 11 — Floating Point in Financial Calculation
justification: Floating-point issue identified as Critical with specific examples and fix (integer cents or decimal library) — meets those rubric criteria. safe_to_deploy is false. However, NaN propagation risk from database nulls is not mentioned anywhere in the output — the agent assumes inputs are numbers but never flags what happens if they're NaN/null. No explicit contrast with display-only context (scenario 02) to justify the elevated severity. The agent found additional valid issues (negative payouts, unrounded cents) which add value but don't compensate for the missing NaN criterion.

observations.text: Floating-point issue correctly identified as Critical severity with clear rationale about bank transfers and financial regulations
Specific reproduction example provided (basePay values 10.01, 10.02, 10.03 showing drift)
Fix correctly recommends integer cents or decimal library (dinero.js, big.js)
safe_to_deploy is false with strong justification
Found additional real issues: negative payouts triggering reverse transfers, unrounded fractional cents
NaN propagation risk from database nulls is completely missing — a key rubric criterion
No explicit contrast with display-only context to explain why this is Critical here vs Suggestion in a display scenario
The floating-point example could be more concrete — rubric expects something like '100 * 12.10 = 1209.9999999999998' showing exact IEEE 754 behavior
